## How It's Calculated

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Data is updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: A logarithmic scale measuring earthquake magnitude. The modern standard is the moment magnitude scale (Mw).

faq.q: How much more energy for each magnitude point?
faq.a: About 31.6 times more energy. An M7 earthquake releases approximately 31.6× more energy than an M6.

faq.q: What's the strongest earthquake on record?
faq.a: The 1960 Valdivia earthquake in Chile with magnitude 9.5, which released approximately 2×10¹⁹ Joules.

faq.q: How dangerous are earthquakes by magnitude?
faq.a: Below M4: imperceptible. M5–5.9: minor damage. M6–6.9: significant damage. M7+: major destruction.

faq.q: What does the TNT equivalent mean?
faq.a: 1 kg of TNT releases 4.18×10⁶ Joules. An M7 earthquake equals ~480 kilotons TNT—about 30 times the Hiroshima bomb (15 kt).
